title: In Memoriam George Warrington Steevens
content:
London, December 10, 1869.
    Ladysmith, January 15, 1900.

    We cheered you forth - brilliant and kind and brave.
    Under your country's triumphing flag you fell.
    It floats, true Heart, over no dearer grave - 
    Brave and brilliant and kind, hail and farewell!
meaning:
This evocative piece by William Ernest Henley, titled "In Memoriam George Warrington Steevens", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
